title: Lomaridium binervatum
taxonomy: Lomaridium binervatum belongs to the family Hymenophyllaceae, which is a family of small to medium-sized, delicate ferns often found in tropical regions. The genus Lomaridium is part of the subclass Polypodiidae and the class Polypodiopsida. This fern is native to tropical Asia and is characterized by its small, binervate leaves. The species is also known to be epiphytic, meaning it grows on other plants for support. Further classification includes its placement in the order Hymenophyllales and the phylum Pteridophyta.
uses: Lomaridium binervatum has been reported to have antimicrobial properties and is traditionally used in folk medicine. It is used to treat various ailments, including fever, and has been employed in the treatment of skin infections and wounds. The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit antibacterial activity, which may contribute to its therapeutic effects. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal dosage for medicinal use. Further studies are also required to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits and potential side effects.
